pattern: Building a shared prefix trie and noting that a prefix depth only becomes unusable for a removed word when that word is the sole reason exactly k words reach it lets every word's answer fall back from the global best only when it was truly indispensable.
hints: Insert every word into a trie and, at each node, count how many words pass through it; any depth where more than k words share a prefix survives the removal of any single word, so it is always a safe answer for everyone.

A depth is only ever at risk for a specific word when exactly k words pass through that exact trie node and the word in question is one of them -- removing a word that never touches such a node never lowers its answer below the safe floor.

Process the risky nodes from deepest to shallowest: every word not among that node's exact k members can immediately claim this depth as its (best possible) answer, while the node's own members must keep looking further up the trie.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, two repeated words dominate
statement example, no two words share any prefix
k equals the total number of words, so any removal drops below k
exactly k words share the deepest prefix, so those specific words must fall back
more than k words share a prefix, making that depth safe for everyone
two different exact-k groups at the same depth so no single word blocks it for the other group

stress.description: random short lowercase words over a tiny alphabet so many share prefixes
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 1500))
    words = []
    for _ in range(n):
        length = rng.randint(1, 6)
        words.append(''.join(rng.choice('ab') for _ in range(length)))
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [words, k]
